mnemonics: Old Tech is a COMPACTOR: Costs, Obstruction, Money, Pollution, And Creates Tons Of Refuse.
keyPoints: Backward compatibility forces continued support for energy-inefficient legacy systems and hardware.
It creates a large volume of electronic waste (e-waste) as outdated components must be maintained and eventually discarded.
It hinders the adoption of newer, more energy-efficient technologies and sustainable practices.
commonMistakes: Assuming backward compatibility is always a positive feature without considering its environmental cost.
Believing that keeping old hardware running is always 'greener' than replacing it with a modern, efficient alternative.
